title: Murder on the Midnight Menu
tagline: At Nocturne, the final course is plated at midnight. Tonight, it serves a corpse.

victim.name: Chef Valentin Voss
victim.role: celebrity chef and owner of Nocturne

solution.fatalMethod: A measured dose of bitter almond liqueur concentrate hidden in Voss’s special Moonless Torte ganache.

solutionExplanation: Bianca Brûlée killed Chef Valentin Voss because he stole The Moonless Torte, planned to sell it for millions, and intended to remove her name from the recipe. She used her dessert-service access to rewrite the final ticket, enter the private plating station, and prepare Voss's separate chef's portion with a fatal dose of bitter almond liqueur concentrate hidden in the ganache. The B.B. black book page proves the theft, while Mina's sighting, the chef's portion note, the rewritten ticket, and the handwriting match complete the opportunity chain.
openingNarration: Nocturne opens at midnight, because Valentin Voss believes dinner should feel like trespass.
Critics, investors, rivals, staff, and loyal regulars gather beneath black chandeliers while Voss prepares one final revelation: The Midnight Menu.
His toast is interrupted before he can name the creator of the final dessert. Then the special chef's portion of The Moonless Torte reaches him behind the applause.
Voss tastes it, smiles once, and collapses beside the gold service bell. The doors are locked. Everyone had motive. Only one person had the final course.
timeline: 10:45 PM — Guests arrive; Marcel quietly changes seating and sells one last impossible reservation.
11:15 PM — Voss argues with Rufus, Celeste, and Gordon in separate corners of the dining room.
11:40 PM — Mina sees Bianca enter the private plating station before dessert service.
11:50 PM — The dessert ticket is rewritten; Voss's chef's portion is marked separately.
12:00 AM — Voss begins an interrupted toast about The Midnight Menu.
12:07 AM — Voss eats the special chef's portion of The Moonless Torte and collapses.

suspects.name: Marcel Pepperidge
suspects.short: Silk gloves, perfect posture, and panic hidden under hospitality.
suspects.secret: Sold impossible reservation spots for cash. Voss planned to fire him publicly.

interview.a: I maintain the room. I do not poison it. Voss humiliated people for sport; tonight he simply ran out of victims.
Chef Voss was brilliant, cruel, and allergic to gratitude. He planned to make an example of me during the toast.
Yes, he intended to fire me. Publicly. But a fired maître d’ still breathes. A murdered chef ruins every reservation in the book.
I was in the wine cellar calming a collector who disliked his table. Ask anyone who values Bordeaux more than truth.
It was Voss's private scripture. He guarded that black book more carefully than the safe.
I changed the seating because Voss ordered it. He wanted enemies close enough to watch one another bleed.

interview.q: Confront Marcel with the cellar service key
interview.a: Every maître d’ knows the quiet doors. That does not mean I crossed into pastry during the final course.

suspects.name: Bianca Brûlée
suspects.short: Calm, exacting, and furious beneath the sugar.
suspects.secret: Voss stole her dessert, The Moonless Torte.
suspects.motive: Voss planned to erase her name and sell her recipe.

interview.a: Dessert is precision. Murder is mess. I prefer precision.
He took beautiful things and signed them with his own name. People called it genius because he wore a white jacket.
Every chef at Nocturne has lost something to Voss. I am not unique in that.
I was on dessert service. Everyone knows that. Access is not guilt.
The black book records ownership. Voss feared honest attribution more than bad reviews.

interview.q: Confront Bianca with the handwriting match
interview.a: Many pastry chefs write neatly. But yes — the ticket resembles mine. You still need to prove what it changed.

interview.q: Confront Bianca with the B.B. page
interview.a: B.B. is me. The Moonless Torte was mine before Voss dressed it in black velvet and called it legacy.

suspects.name: Rufus Kale
suspects.short: A velvet voice with a knife behind every adjective.
suspects.motive: Voss had proof and planned to expose him.

interview.a: I kill restaurants with stars, not toxins.
Voss was a thief with plating tweezers. Unfortunately, he was also useful copy.
He had nothing on me that half the industry did not already suspect.
Dining room, table three. I was composing a review cruel enough to outlive him.
A myth, supposedly. Recipes, debts, affairs, bribes — all reduced to culinary metaphor.
Fine. He had proof I sold praise. That gave me motive, not access to the chef's private dessert.

interview.q: Confront Rufus with the kitchen pass
interview.a: Voss gave me access for a private preview, not plating rights. I saw the kitchen door, not the final plate.

suspects.name: Celeste Saffron
suspects.short: Gold jewellery, red lipstick, and the calm of someone who has survived audits.
suspects.secret: Used money from a failed restaurant group she stripped for parts.
suspects.motive: Voss planned to remove her and expose her finances.

interview.a: I invested in Nocturne. I did not invest in scandal.
Valentin mistook ownership for loyalty. Once he had money, he wanted witnesses gone.
He threatened to remove me. Investors are removed by contracts, not corpses.
Wine cellar, briefly, then the dining room. Marcel can confirm part of it if he is feeling brave.
It was leverage. Voss wrote finances as recipes and called blackmail cuisine.
He planned to expose the old restaurant group. Yes. But killing him during my own opening night would be an ugly return on investment.

suspects.name: Gordon Glaze
suspects.short: A wounded ego in a tailored jacket.
suspects.secret: Helped create the dish that made Voss famous.
suspects.motive: Voss stole his work and ruined his restaurant.

interview.a: If I wanted Voss dead, I would have challenged him to cook honestly.
He built an empire from other people's hands. Mine included.
He stole my dish and my future. I wanted him exposed, not martyred.
Wine cellar. I needed distance before I threw a glass at him in front of critics.
If the black book exists, it contains more stolen work than a bad cookbook shelf.
Yes, Voss stole from me. But tonight's dessert was not mine. Ask the pastry station.

suspects.name: Mina Mise
suspects.short: Young, sharp-eyed, and frightened by how much she noticed.
suspects.secret: Copied pages from The Midnight Menu for weeks.

interview.a: I only wanted the truth copied somewhere he could not burn it.
He destroyed my father's restaurant with a review he bought and a rumour he served cold.
I hated him. I did not touch his plate.
Kitchen pass until dessert. I saw Bianca go behind the velvet curtain before the final course.
I copied pages because the book was evidence. Recipes, ledgers, threats — all hidden as tasting notes.

interview.q: Confront Mina with the missing black book
interview.a: I copied pages, not the whole book. Someone took the original after Voss fell.

suspects.name: Dahlia Plate
suspects.short: A camera smile, trembling hands, and too many drafts saved offline.
suspects.secret: Her fame was manufactured by Voss with scripted reviews.
suspects.motive: Voss planned to leak proof her career was fake.

interview.a: My followers saw the toast cut off. They did not see murder. I wish my phone had kept recording.
He made me famous, then owned every word I said about food.
He threatened to leak scripts. I would be finished, but prison is worse than cancellation.
Back hallway, fixing a livestream glitch. The restored clip proves where I was.
Everyone whispered about it. Voss treated secrets like ingredients.
The clip shows Bianca passing the plating curtain. I did not understand it until now.

interview.q: Confront Dahlia with the edited timestamp
interview.a: I cut the clip because Voss sounded cruel in it. I did not realise the missing seconds made me look like the murderer.

suspects.name: Arthur Spoon
suspects.short: An old man with soup opinions and investigator's eyes.
suspects.motive: Voss discovered him and threatened to frame him.

interview.a: Never trust a restaurant that serves foam before soup.
A fraud with a tasting menu. I came to prove it, not kill it.
He discovered my investigation and threatened to frame me with his own ledger. Tiresome, but not fatal.
Dining room. I was watching the service pattern. The murderer moved with permission, not stealth.
The black recipe book is a coded ledger. Courses are crimes; ingredients are names.

interview.q: Confront Arthur with the coded ledger
interview.a: The book could ruin half this room. That is why its disappearance matters more than the body.

locations.name: Dining Room
locations.purpose: Opening scene, guest statements, missing recipe book clue.
locations.description: Black marble tables, cream linen, gold cutlery, and critics pretending not to panic.

locations.name: Kitchen
locations.purpose: Staff gossip, service ticket clue, Bianca/Mina related clues.
locations.description: A hot line of copper pans, sharp knives, and staff who suddenly remember nothing clearly.

locations.name: Private Plating Station
locations.purpose: Chef's portion clue, Bianca opportunity clue.
locations.description: A velvet-curtained station where the final course was finished away from guest eyes.

locations.name: Voss’s Office
locations.purpose: Coded ledger clue, investor deal clue, Arthur investigation clue.
locations.description: A lacquered office above the kitchen, smelling of ink, truffle oil, and blackmail.

locations.name: Wine Cellar
locations.purpose: Red herring location for Marcel, Celeste, and Gordon.
locations.description: Dusty bottles, old money, and three separate alibis too convenient to be clean.

locations.name: Back Hallway
locations.description: A narrow staff corridor where camera glitches and footsteps become evidence.

clues.title: Missing black recipe book
clues.text: Voss's black recipe book vanished after dessert. It may contain recipes and coded crimes.

clues.title: Voss’s interrupted toast
clues.text: Voss began a toast about authorship, legacy, and The Midnight Menu before someone cut the moment short.

clues.title: Rewritten dessert ticket
clues.text: The final dessert ticket was rewritten after service began, changing the handling of Voss's chef's portion.

clues.title: Pastry-station handwriting match
clues.text: The rewritten ticket matches the precise decorative hand used on pastry-station labels.

clues.title: B.B. black book page
clues.text: A torn black book page reads: 'Original author: B.B. Remove name.' It refers to The Moonless Torte.

clues.title: Chef’s portion note
clues.text: Voss received a special chef's portion, plated separately from the guests' desserts.

clues.title: Mina saw Bianca enter plating station
clues.text: Mina saw Bianca enter the private plating station before the final course.

clues.title: Dahlia’s restored video clip
clues.text: Dahlia’s corrupted hallway clip shows movement near the plating curtain before dessert. It may have been edited before recovery.

clues.title: Moonless Torte authorship dispute
clues.text: Draft notes show The Moonless Torte existed before Voss announced it as his own final-course masterpiece.

clues.title: Million-dollar dessert sale
clues.text: A draft deal shows Voss planned to sell The Moonless Torte concept for millions under his own name.

clues.title: Rufus bribery page
clues.text: A coded black-book page links Rufus to paid reviews.

clues.title: Celeste investor threat
clues.text: Voss prepared documents to remove Celeste and expose her stripped restaurant-group finances.

clues.title: Gordon recipe theft motive
clues.text: Old notes show Gordon helped create the dish that made Voss famous before Voss ruined him.

clues.title: Arthur discovered the book was a coded ledger
clues.text: Arthur identified the black recipe book as a ledger of bribes, thefts, threats, and fraud.

clues.title: Marcel changed the seating plan
clues.text: Marcel changed the opening-night seating plan and sold impossible reservation spots for cash.

clues.title: Bitter almond ganache trace
clues.text: A scrap from Voss’s private plate smells sharply of bitter almond liqueur concentrate, hidden under dark chocolate ganache.

clues.title: Cellar service key
clues.text: A service key found near the wine cellar could open the back hallway door, giving Marcel, Celeste, or Gordon a suspicious movement route.

clues.title: Critic kitchen pass
clues.text: A one-night kitchen pass issued to Rufus suggests he briefly had permission to approach the pass, though not the private plating station.

clues.title: Edited video timestamp
clues.text: Dahlia’s restored clip has a timestamp jump, making her look suspicious before the full recovery shows Bianca near plating.

motive.label: Voss stole The Moonless Torte and planned to sell it while erasing Bianca's name.
Voss planned to publicly fire Marcel.
Voss planned to expose Rufus for paid reviews.
Voss planned to expose Celeste's finances.
Voss stole Gordon's work and ruined his restaurant.
Voss planned to expose Dahlia's fake fame.

opportunity.label: Bianca had dessert-service access and entered the private plating station before the final course.
Someone slipped out from the wine cellar during the confusion.
The poison was added publicly during Voss's toast.
The black book triggered a trap in Voss's office.

supportingClues.label: Mina saw Bianca enter plating station
Arthur discovered the book was a coded ledger

expansionNotes: Add generated art by replacing placeholder paths in imagePlaceholders.
Add more interview depth by appending question objects to each suspect's interview array.
Add multi-act pacing by adding round/stage fields to clues and locations.
